scope: Assess which official Figma MCP capabilities align with AutoDesign's product vision and should enter the active roadmap.
measurements: Reviewed 16 official MCP tool families plus the high-level capability overview on March 25, 2026.
8 capability families align directly with the product vision.
2 capability families are only weakly aligned and should remain downgraded or deferred.
6 capability families should stay out of the current active roadmap.
findings: The aligned scope is the local equivalent of design-context extraction, variable and metadata reads, screenshot capture, design-system mapping, and a safe subset of use_figma write operations.
Remote hosted MCP, whoami, FigJam, Make resources, code-to-canvas, and blank-file provisioning are not current product-line priorities.
The current gap is capability-surface completeness, not the absence of the MCP label.
follow_up: Promote the aligned subset into a dedicated active roadmap item.
Implement the work in phased order: read/context parity, design-system parity, then safe write parity.
